title: Design brief + glyph set spec for low-vision/dyslexia-informed Latin family
context: M1 is the accessibility-first Latin family, chosen first because it is partner-light (no script community gate). Before any glyph drawing begins, the project needs a design brief and glyph-set specification that translates the accessibility evidence brief (fonts-research-005) and governance framework (fonts-gov-006) into concrete type-design requirements. The brief must remain within non-medical framing and the honesty constraints established in M0.
objective: Produce a design brief and glyph set specification for the accessibility-first Latin family, covering design rationale, the GF Latin Core glyph set plus the accessibility-critical differentiation set, axis strategy (variable weight at minimum), and the mandated non-medical framing text to appear in all family artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Design brief references and applies the accessibility evidence brief (fonts-research-005) findings without over-claiming clinical or medical benefit.
Glyph set specification covers GF Latin Core and the accessibility-critical differentiation set: distinct, non-confusable forms for I/l/1, O/0, b/d/p/q, rn/m, and other high-confusion pairs.
Brief specifies the variable-axis strategy: at minimum a weight axis; optical-size axis if justified, with rationale stated.
Brief includes the mandated non-medical framing text to appear in README, specimens, and usage guide.
Brief addresses original clean-room drawing vs. OFL-derivative approach and records the decision with license rationale.
Brief is reviewed and approved by the maintainer and, once secured, an accessibility/vision specialist; sign-off recorded.
Document is CC-BY-4.0; no proprietary reference material is incorporated.
resources: fonts-research-005 (accessibility evidence brief — must be completed first)
output: A design brief and glyph-set specification document (CC-BY-4.0) for the accessibility-first Latin family, covering design rationale, glyph repertoire, variable-axis strategy, and non-medical framing text, merged into the repository.
